nameMeanings.name: Oliphant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London) and Scottish (Fife): from Old French olifard or olifant, of uncertain meaning. It has been suggested that this might be a derivative of Old French olif ‘olive’ + the pejorative suffix -ard ‘derisive nickname for one who preferred an olive branch to more martial weapons’; it may have denoted a man twisted like an olive-tree. The variant Olifant may have resulted from the substitution in Old Norman French of the sequence l-r by l-n, leading to a false association with Old French olifant ‘elephant’. This heraldic beast appears in the Scottish family's coats of arms. Branches of the Anglo-Norman family owned lands in both England and Scotland. The surname is now chiefly Scottish.
English: possibly a topographic name from residence at an inn known as the Olyphaunt or ‘elephant’, though whether this gave rise to a hereditary surname is unknown.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Oliphant co-heiress of Dron was born about 1415, in Scotland. She married Henry Wardlaw. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 12 February 1511, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland, at the age of 97.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Oliphant co-heiress of Dron est née environ 1415, à Écosse. Elle a épousé Henry Wardlaw. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 février 1511, à Aberdeenshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 97 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Oliphant co-heiress of Dron wurde um 1415 in Scotland geboren. Sie hat Henry Wardlaw geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Februar 1511 im Alter von 97 Jahren in Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Oliphant co-heiress of Dron è nata il circa 1415 nel Scotland. Lei sposato Henry Wardlaw. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 12 febbraio 1511, nel Aberdeenshire, Scotland, all'età di 97 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Oliphant co-heiress of Dron(은)는 대략 1415년 스컷랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Henry Wardlaw(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1511년 2월 12일 스컷랜드 압르디실르에서 97세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Oliphant co-heiress of Dron nasceu aproximadamente 1415, em Scotland. Ela casou-se com Henry Wardlaw. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 12 de fevereiro de 1511, em Aberdeenshire, Scotland, com 97 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Oliphant co-heiress of Dron родилась около 1415 г. в местности Шотландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Wardlaw. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 12 февраля 1511 г. в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия в возрасте 97 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Oliphant co-heiress of Dron nació aproximadamente 1415, en Scotland. Se casó con Henry Wardlaw. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 12 de febrero de 1511, en Aberdeenshire, Scotland, a la edad de 97 años.
